home.subtitle: DemocracyConnect makes learning about the world's largest democratic exercise simple, interactive, and fun.

features.timeline_desc: Explore the step-by-step process of how elections are conducted in India.
features.learn_desc: Master important election terminology using interactive flashcards.
features.quiz_desc: Take a fun quiz to see how much you know about Indian democracy.
features.chatbot_desc: Have questions? Chat with our AI assistant to get instant answers.

timeline.subtitle: Step-by-step guide to how elections are conducted in the world's largest democracy.
timeline.step1_desc: The Election Commission of India (ECI) announces the schedule, and the Model Code of Conduct comes into effect.
timeline.step2_desc: Candidates file their nomination papers along with an affidavit detailing their assets and criminal records.
timeline.step3_desc: The Returning Officer scrutinizes the nominations. Valid candidates get a few days to withdraw if they choose to.
timeline.step4_desc: Candidates campaign to win over voters. Campaigning stops 48 hours before polling begins.
timeline.step5_desc: Citizens vote using Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs). A VVPAT slip confirms the vote was cast correctly.
timeline.step6_desc: Votes are counted on a scheduled day under strict security, and the results are declared.
